task_completion.description: Log into the OpenMRS Administration interface
task_completion.visual_evidence: The OpenMRS Administration dashboard is visible, indicating successful login (expected user: superman).

task_completion.description: Navigate to the Scheduler or Manage Tasks section
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Manage Scheduler Tasks' page or a list of scheduled tasks is visible in the OpenMRS interface.

task_completion.description: Open the configuration for the HL7 Inbound Queue Processor task
task_completion.visual_evidence: The configuration or edit page specifically for the 'HL7 In Queue Processor' (or 'HL7 Inbound Queue Processor') task is open.

task_completion.description: Update the Repeat Interval to 300 seconds
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Repeat Interval' input field contains the value '300'.
A success message (e.g., 'Task saved') is visible, or the task list is shown with the 'HL7 In Queue Processor' repeat interval updated to 300.

integrity.description: Agent used a web browser to interact with the OpenMRS GUI
integrity.visual_evidence: A web browser window is visible navigating to the OpenMRS URL, rather than using curl or API testing tools.

integrity.description: Agent did not bypass the GUI using direct database commands
integrity.visual_evidence: There are no terminal windows showing SQL commands (like UPDATE scheduler_task_config) being executed to change the interval.

integrity.description: Agent interacted with the genuine OpenMRS application
integrity.visual_evidence: The screenshots show the actual OpenMRS web interface with proper styling and layout, not a text editor or mock HTML file.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The OpenMRS Admin interface is located at https://localhost/openmrs/admin. The credentials are Username: superman, Password: Admin123. The target repeat interval is 300 seconds. Note: While the task description mentions 'HL7 Inbound Queue Processor', the actual task name in the OpenMRS UI is typically 'HL7 In Queue Processor'.
